title: JavaScript JSX Source
documentation:
## Purpose
JSX files contain JavaScript with embedded XML-like syntax extensions (JSX) used primarily with the React library to describe user interface trees. JSX is a compile-time syntax transformation and is not valid plain JavaScript.

## When to use
Use [JavaScript JSX Source](https://capdag.com/browse/media:jsx;textable;code) for React component files with the `.jsx` extension. If the file is TypeScript with JSX, use [TypeScript JSX Source](https://capdag.com/browse/media:tsx;textable;code) instead. Plain JavaScript without JSX should use [JavaScript Source](https://capdag.com/browse/media:javascript;textable;code).

## Notes
JSX must be transformed to standard JavaScript before execution, typically via Babel, esbuild, or the TypeScript compiler. The JSX transform can target React's `createElement` API (classic) or the newer automatic runtime introduced in React 17. Linters and formatters must be configured to understand JSX syntax.
